title: An Insurance Rate Hike In Frankfurt
text:
An Insurance Rate Hike In Frankfurt

Cedric Gemehl, August Gudmundsson

Gavekal Research | 9 Jun 2026

The European Central Bank will kick off a round of major developed-market central bank meetings when it convenes on Thursday, ahead of the Bank of Japan, Federal Reserve and Bank of England, all of which meet next week. Investors will be looking for guidance on how policymakers intend to respond to a negative supply shock in energy markets which is pushing inflation up and economic growth down.There is little suspense around the ECB’s expected decision to raise its deposit rate from 2% to 2.25% after a year on hold. The real question is what comes next. Investors will scrutinize a fresh set of projections and president Christine Lagarde’s press conference for clues as to whether this is merely an insurance rate hike in response to an adverse inflation shock, or the start of a broader tightening cycle.
